Which number should be added to 3, 11, 4 and 14 to make them in proportion?
When x is added to each of the numbers 7, 11, 18 and 23, then the number so obtained are in proportion. What is the mean proportional between the (x - 1) and (2x - 10)?
When x is added to each of 2, 3, 30 and 35, then the numbers obtained in this order, are in proportion. What is the mean proportional between (x + 7) & (x - 2)?
If x is subtracted from each of 24, 40, 33 and 57, the numbers, so obtained are in proportion. The ratio of (5x + 12) to (4x + 15) is
When x is subtracted from each of 24, 33, 20 and 26, the number so obtained in this order are in proportion. What is the mean proportional between (2x + 1) and (5x - 11)?
If four distinct positive numbers, a, b, c and d in the order given are in proportion then which of the following options is NOT correct?

options.A: b, a, d and c, in the order given are in proportion
options.B: c, d, b and a, in the order given are in proportion.
options.C: d, c, b and a, in the order given are in proportion.
options.D: a, c, b and d, in the order given are in proportion.

question: Four distinct positive numbers, 'a', 'b', 'c' and 'd', in the order given, are in proportion. 'b' is 35 more than 'a' and 'd' is 60 more than 'c'. The product of 'a' and 'c' is 5376. What is the sum of 'a', 'b', 'c' and 'd'?
If x varies inversely as y and x = 2 when y = 6, then the value of y when x = 3 is:
If a ∝ b, b ∝ 1/c and c ∝ d, then the relation between a and d is:
M is inversely proportional to N. If M is 20, then N is 70. If N = 50, then what is the value of M?
A varies directly as (B + 18) and A = 108 when B = 36 Find the value of A when B = 68
A varies directly with B. When A = 52, B = 91. Find the value of A when B = 126.
M varies inversely as (N² + 3). If M = 3 when N = 3, then what will be the value of M when N² = 69?
A varies directly as the positive square root of B, and inversely as the cube of C. If A = 15, when B = 27 and C = 2, then find B when A = 9 and C = 2.
A's marks in Mathematics are directly proportional to practice time. In 6 hours of practice, A gets 70 marks. What should be the practice time (approximately) to get 90 marks?
A function of two variable varies directly with x and inversely with y. Determine function when x = 5 and y = 3. Given that, for x = 0 and y = 1, f = 15 and for x = 1 and y = 15, f = 2.
'x' varies directly with the cube of 'y', and inversely with the square of 'z'. If x = 1/36 when y = 2 and z = 3, then what is the value of 800x when y = 3 and z = 5?
y is directly proportional to x + 3 and y = 8 when x = 1, x inversely proportional to z and x = 2 when z = 6 Find the relation between y and z.

The age of a tree depends on the number of rings in the trunk. The value of a such tree is proportional to its age. If a tree with 40 rings and 50 years of age has a value of ₹50,000, then a tree with 30 rings is its value is ______ years of age and its value is ₹ ______.
The falling height of an object is directly proportional to the square of the time. An object falls 80 cm in 4 seconds. Then in 8 seconds, from how much height (in cm) will the object fall?
What smallest positive number should be subtracted from 2 and 3 so that 4 is the third proportion to them?

The weight of a circular disc varies directly as the product of the square of the radius and its thickness. Two identical/similar discs have their thickness in the ratio of 16 : 9. What is the ratio of their radii if the weight of the first is four times that of the second?

The weight of a cube varies directly as the product of its volume and its density. The ratio of densities of the materials of the first cube and second cube is 27 : 16. If the weight of the first cube is 4 times the weight of the second cube, then what is the ratio of the edges of the first cube and second cube?
The ages of Fatima and Ahmed are in the ratio of 3:8. The sum of their present ages is 44 years. The difference of their ages is:
The ratio of a father's age to his son's age is 3:2. The product of the numbers representing their age is 486. The ratio of their ages after 5 years will be:
The ratio of a father's age to his son's age is 7 : 4. The product of the numbers representing their ages is 1372. The ratio of their ages after 8 years will be:
The sum of the present ages of a father and son is 50 years. If after 5 years, the father's age will be 5 times the age of the son, then what was the father's age 5 years ago?
The sum of the ages of a son and father is 52 years; After four years the father's age will be four times the son's age. What is the present age (in years) of the son?
The sum of the ages of 6 children born at the interval of two years each is 90 years. What is the age (in years) of the eldest child?
Hema is 20 years old and her sister Neha is 30 years old. How many years ago were their ages in the ratio of 3:5?
Nihira is 25 years old and Punith is 30 years old. How many years ago was the ratio of their ages 3 : 4?
The present ages (in years) of A and B are in the ratio of 3 : 4. Five years ago, the ratio of their ages was 2 : 3. What is the present age (in years) of A?
At present, A is thrice as old as B. C is 5 years older than B. The sum of the ages of A, B, C is 75 years. The ratio of the age of B five years ago to the age of A three years from now is:
Ghanshyam is thrice as old as Akash. 10 years ago, Ghanshyam was four times as old as Akash. What is the difference between their current ages (in years)?
My father is presently 25 years older than me. The sum of our ages 5 years ago was 39 years. Find my present age.
A father is presently 3 times his daughter's age. After 10 years he will be twice as old as her. Find the daughter's present age.
In the year 2000, Monu was 3 times his sister's age. In 2010, he was 24 years older than her. Find Monu's age in 2010.
The age of the older of two boys is thrice that of the younger. 9 years ago it was five times that of the younger. Find the present age of each.
The ages of A and B are in the ratio 4 : 7, respectively. After 8 years, the ratio of their ages will be 2 : 3. What is the sum of the ages of A and B (in years)?
The ratio of the ages of A and B is 3 : 4. Four years ago, the ratio of their ages was 5 : 7. What will be the ratio of the ages of A and B four years from now?
The present ages of A and B are in the ratio 3 : 4. Twelve years ago, their ages were in the ratio 2 : 3. The sum of the present ages of A and B (in years) is:
The ratio of present ages (in years) of a father and son is 15 : 8. Six years ago, the ratio of their ages was 13 : 6. What is the father's present age?
Amit's father is aged five times more than Amit. After 6 years, he would be three and a half times of Amit's age. After further 9 years, how many times of Amit's age would he be?

If the age of a person is four times the sum of the ages of his three children, and after 6 years his age will be twice the sum of the ages of his three children, then what is the present age of that person?
The present ages of Ramu and Ravi are in the ratio of 4 : 3. After 5 years the ratio of their ages will be 5 : 4. Find their present ages, respectively
Three boys, on an average, are 25 years old, and their age are in the ratio of 3 : 5 : 7. What is the age of the youngest boy?
A family, consists of a father, mother and son. The ratio of their present ages is 8 : 5 : 2 and the present age of the father is 48 years. What will be the average age of family after 3 years ?
The average age of A, B and C is 20 years. Two years ago the sum of the ages of A and B was 6 more than that of C. Find the present age of C?
The average age of Ruby and Soni is 40 years. The ratio of their ages is 11 : 5, respectively. What is the age (in years) of Soni?

The current average age of a family of five members is 24. If the present age of the youngest member in the family is 8 years, what was the average age of the family members just before the birth of this youngest member?
8 years ago, the average age of Rahul's family consisting of 6 members was 35 years. One year ago a new baby was born in this family. What will be the average age of the family four years hence?
Six years ago, the average of the ages of Ravi, Mohan and Govind was 32 years. If Shyam joins them now, the average of the ages of all four of them is 36 years. The present age of Shyam is:

type: TYPE-III - Based on Difference of Age
question: Seven years ago, the ratio of the ages of A and B was 4 : 5. Eight years hence, the ratio of the ages of A and B will be 9 : 10. What is the difference between their present ages in years?

type: TYPE-III - Based on Difference of Age
question: The ratio of the ages of A and B, four years ago, was 4 : 5. Eight years from now, the ratio of the ages of A and B will be 11 : 13. What is the sum of their present ages?

type: TYPE-III - Based on Difference of Age
question: The ratio of the present age of two girls is 3 : 4. After two year the ratio will be 7 : 9. Which of the following statement (s) is/are correct? I. Ratio of their ages after 10 year is 6 : 7. II. Ratio of their ages after 12 years is 6 : 7.

type: TYPE-III - Based on Difference of Age
question: 10 years ago, the age of Sunita was thrice the age of her daughter Tanya. 10 years hence, the age of Sunita will be twice the age of Tanya. What is the ratio of their present ages?

type: TYPE-III - Based on Difference of Age
question: Four years ago, Ravi was four times as old as Kavya. Seven years from now, Ravi will be three times as old as Kavya. Find the sum of their present ages.

type: TYPE-III - Based on Difference of Age
question: One year ago, the age of B was twice that of A. After 9 years, if the ratio of the ages of B and A would be 4 : 3, then the sum of their present ages is ____ years.

type: TYPE-III - Based on Difference of Age
question: The ratio of the ages of A and B, 3 years ago, was 5 : 7. The ratio of their ages, 13 years from now, will be 9 : 11. If the present age of C is 11 years less than that of B, then the present age of C, in years, is:

type: TYPE-III - Based on Difference of Age
question: One year ago, the ratio of the age (in years) of A to that of B was 4:3. The ratio of their respective ages, 3 years from now, will be 6:5. What will be the ratio of respective ages of A and B, 9 years from now?

type: TYPE-III - Based on Difference of Age
question: The age of a father will be double the age of his son ten years later. 10 years ago, the father's age was six times the age of the son. How many years from now, the ratio of their ages will be 3 : 2?

type: TYPE-III - Based on Difference of Age
question: Seven years ago, Prachi was four times as old as her daughter was at that time. Four years from now, Prachi will be two-and-a-half times as old as her daughter would then be. Find the sum of the present ages (in years) of Prachi and her daughter.

type: TYPE-III - Based on Difference of Age
question: Three years ago, the age of a person was six times the age of his younger brother. Eight years later, the age of the person will be three years less than twice the age of his younger brother. Find the present ages of the person and his younger brother.

type: TYPE-III - Based on Difference of Age
question: 5 years ago, the ratio of the age of A to that of B was 4:5. Five years hence, the ratio of the age of A to that of B will be 6:7. If, at present, C is 10 years younger than B, then what will be the ratio of the present age of A to that of C ?

type: TYPE-III - Based on Difference of Age
question: The ratio of the ages of A and B, 8 years ago, was 5 : 7. The ratio of their ages, 8 years from now, will be 9 : 11. If the present age of C is 13 years less than that of B, and the present age of D is 8 years less than that of the age of A, then the sum of the present ages of C and D, in years, is:

type: TYPE-III - Based on Difference of Age
question: 10 years ago, a man's age was 5 times of his son's age. 2 years hence, twice his age will be equal to 4 times the age of his son. What is the present age (in years) of the son?

type: TYPE-III - Based on Difference of Age
question: Ram is 20 years older than Rahul. 'n' years ago Ram was thrice as old as Rahul. '2n' years from now the ratio of the ages of Ram and Rahul will be 15 : 11. What is the value of 'n' ?

type: TYPE-III - Based on Difference of Age
question: The ratio of the ages of two friends is 4 : 3 which will become 6 : 5 after 4 years. What will be the sum of their ages (in years) after 22 years from now?
The present ages of Sweeta and Suman are in the ratio 4 : 5. Six years back, the ratio of their ages was 2 : 3. What is the present age of Sweeta?

The ages of two friends, Ram and Ravi, differ by 7 years. Ram's mother Mayadevi is three times as old as Ram, and Ravi is four times as old as his brother Soham. The ages of Mayadevi and Soham differ by 65 years. Find the age of Mayadevi (in years).

In a family, the combined age of husband, wife and a son is 90 years. The husband is the oldest. The difference in the ages of the husband and wife is 1/3rd the age of their son. After 10 years, the age of the son would be half the age of his father. What is the present age of his mother (in years)?

P got married 15 years ago. Today her age is 1 3/5 times her age at the time of her marriage. At present her son's age is 1/5 of her age. What was her son's age 4 years ago as a fraction of P's age at that time?

Anil and Beena are friends, and the difference between their ages is 5 years. Anil's father Dinesh is three times as old as Anil, and Beena is twice as old as her sister Charu. The ages of Dinesh and Charu differ by 45 years. If Beena is older than Anil, then find the sum of the present ages of both Dinesh and Charu.

The ratio of investment by A to that by B in a business is 14 : 15 and the ratio of their respective profits at the end of a year is 2 : 5. If A invested the money for 3 months, then for how much time (in months) B invested his money?
A, B and C started a business. A's capital is 7 times the capital of C and B's capital is 2 times the capital of A. If the profit is ₹7,920, then find the share of C (in ₹).
A, B and C invested money in a business such that 2 times A's share, 4 times B's share and 6 times C's share are all equal. If they earned Rs.44,000, find the share of A.
P, Q and R are partners and invest in a business. P invests 1/5th of total investments and Q invests 1/4th of the total investments. What is the ratio of investments of P, Q and R respectively?

Three partners P, Q and R invested a total of ₹52,000 in a business. At the end of the year, P got ₹1430, Q got ₹1870 and R got ₹2420 as the share of profit. How much amount did Q invest in the business?
A, B, C invest a sum of ₹75,500 for a business. A invest ₹3,500 more than B, and B invest ₹4,500 more than C. Out of a total profit of ₹45,300, how much (in ₹) does A receive?
Arvind, Bilal and Carmen invest ₹49,000 in a business. Arvind invested ₹5,000 more than Bilal and Bilal invests ₹4,000 more than Carmen out of a total profit of ₹98,000. How much does Bilal receive?

A, B and C start a business. A invests 33 1/3% of the total capital, B invests 25% of the remaining, and C invests the rest. If the total profit at the end of the year is ₹1,86,000, then A's share of the profit (in ₹) is:
A, B and C start a business. A invests 33 1/3% of the total capital, B invests 25% of the remaining and C, the rest. If the total profit at the end of the year is ₹219000, then A's share (in ₹) is:

A, B and C start a business. A invests 33 1/3% of the total capital, B invest 25% of the remaining and C invests the rest. If the total profit at the end of a year is ₹162,000, then A's share in profit is:
A starts a business with ₹45,000 and was joined afterwards by B with ₹30,000. When did B join if the profit at the end of the year was divided in the ratio 2 : 1?
P invested ₹40000 one year. Q joined him with an investment of ₹6000 after a few months. If at the end of one year the profits of P and Q is 4 : 3, then Q joined after how many months?
A starts a business with ₹75,000 and B joins the business 5 months later with an investment of ₹80,000. After 1 year, they earn a profit of ₹4,08,800. Find the share of A and B (in ₹).

type: TYPE-II - When Someone Joined or Left
question: A started a business with B and C at ₹5,000 and continued it for 4 months and 6 months, respectively. At the end of the year, the profit was divided in the ratio 1 : 5. What is the difference between the capitals invested by B and C?

type: TYPE-II - When Someone Joined or Left
question: A and B enter into a partnership with ₹25,000 and ₹50,000, respectively, in a company for a year. After 7 months, C gets into a partnership with them with ₹30,000 and A withdraws his contribution after 6 months. What would be C's share in the profit, assuming a total profit of ₹3,600 at the end of the year?

type: TYPE-II - When Someone Joined or Left
question: A, B and C started a business with the investment of ₹100000, ₹140000 and ₹200000 respectively. After 3 months, C left the business, 7 months later C left the business, B also left the business. B and C took their investments with them. At the end of the year, C received his share of profit as ₹1,55. What is the total share of profits of A and B?

type: TYPE-II - When Someone Joined or Left
question: Kapil initiated a business with an investment of ₹30,00,000 in April 2022. After six months, his friend Kumar joined him with an investment of ₹25,00,000. In February 2023, Kapil's Ex-Class fellow Saloni also invested an amount equal to the total investment made by the earlier two partners. In what ratio the profit earned after 3 years from the date on which Kapil initiated the business, will be distributed?

X and Y enter into a partnership with capital in the ratio 3 : 5. After 5 months X adds 50% of his capital, while Y withdraws 60% of his capital. What is the share (in lakhs) of X in the annual profit of ₹6.84 lakhs?

Suresh, Dinesh and Ramesh became partners in a business by investing money in the ratio of 3 : 6 : 8. If their investments is increased by 5%, 15% and 20%, respectively, then what will be the ratio of their profits for one year?

A, B and C started a business in partnership. Initially, A invested ₹20,000, while B and C invested ₹25,000 each. After 4 months, A withdrew ₹1,000. After 2 more months, C invested ₹12,000 more. Find the share of C (in ₹) in the profit of ₹11,200 at the end of the year.

Three partners X, Y and Z started their business by investing ₹10,000, ₹38,000 and ₹30,000, respectively. After 6 months, X and Z made additional investments of ₹20,000 and ₹15,000 respectively, whereas Y withdrew ₹8,000. Find the share of Y (in ₹) in the total profit of ₹38,880 made at the end of the year.

A, B and C started a business with initial investments of ₹20000, ₹25000 and ₹10000, respectively. After 5 months from start, A invested ₹4000 more. After 6 months from start, C invested ₹8000 more. After 4 months from start, B withdrew ₹8000. At the end of the year, they will receive a profit of ₹x. In what ratio they will share the profits?

A, B and C started a business with their capitals in the ratio 2 : 3 : 5. A increased his capital by 50% after 4 months, B increased his capital by 33 1/3% after 6 months and C withdrew 50% of his capital after 8 months, from the start of the business. If the total profit at the end of a year was ₹86,800, then the difference between the shares of A and C in the profit was:

A, B and C invested ₹40,000, ₹48,000 and ₹80,000, respectively, for a business at the start of a year. After 6 months, for the remaining time of the year, A added ₹4,000, B added ₹4,000 while C withdrew ₹4,000 every month. If the total profit is ₹6,72,000, then what is C's share (in ₹)?

Three people, A, B and C, invest in a business in the ratio 2 : 3 : 5. It was decided that 9% of the profit will go to charity. If the total profit was ₹2,50,000, then find the share of C in the profit (in ₹).
Mayur and Aham enter into a partnership by making investments in the ratio 2 : 3 : 10% of the total profit goes to charity. If Aham's share is ₹4,360, then what is the total profit (in ₹) earned?
A and B invested money in a business in the ratio of 7 : 5. If 15% of the total profit goes for charity and A's share in the profit is ₹5,950, then what is the total profit?

Three partners decided to start a business. They decided to donate 10% of the profit to a charity organisation and then the remaining profit would be shared among first, second and third partner in the ratio 1 : 2 : 2. The total profit received was ₹1,80,000. Find the share of the first partner.

Keshav, Surjeet and Thomas started a business with investments in the ratio 2 : 3 : 4. The ratio of their period of investments is 5 : 6 : 9. Twenty percent of the profit was spent on rent and maintenance of the office. Remaining profit was distributed among themselves. If the difference in the shares of profit of Keshav and Surjeet is ₹72604, then how much is the total profit (in ₹)?

Two friends A and B started a business by investing ₹1,50,000 and ₹2,50,000, respectively. They agreed to distribute their earnings in the same ratio of their investments. After an year, the profit earned was ₹6,00,000. Each of them added ₹50,000 to their respective profits and invested in a different project. If this project gave an yield of ₹4,20,000, then A's share in the profit is:

A is a working partner and B is a sleeping partner in a business. A invested ₹30,000 and B invested ₹50,000. A receives 20% of the profit for managing the business and the rest is divided in proportion to their capital. What does B get out of the profit of ₹10,000.

A, B and C jointly started a business in which A invested ₹7,000 for 12 months, B invested ₹8,000 for 10 months and C invested Rs.12,000 for 6 months. A was the working member for which he received 20% of the profits as his remuneration. The total profit earned at the end of one year was ₹14,750. What is C's share (in ₹) in the profit?

P and Q start a shop with a capital of ₹1,50,000 and ₹4,50,000, respectively. After a year, out of the profit of ₹2,00,000, P gets his share of profit plus some money as his salary that is not a part of the profit. If P gets a total of ₹90,000, what is the amount of salary (in ₹) that he received?

P and Q start a shop with a capital of ₹1,50,000 and ₹4,50,000, respectively. After a year, out of the profit of ₹1,60,000, P gets his share of profit plus some money that is not a part of the profit, as his salary. If P gets a total of ₹70,000, what is the salary (in ₹) he received:

A bottle is filled with liquid of which 4 parts are water and 5 parts are fruit extract. How much of the mixture must be drawn off and replaced with water so that the mixture may be half water and half fruit extract?

A vessel is filled with liquid, 5 parts of which are water and 11 parts syrup. What part of the mixture must be drawn off and replaced with water so that the mixture may have syrup and water in the ratio 3 : 2?

A container contains a mixture of two liquids, A and B, in the proportion 7 : 5. If 9 liters of the mixture is replaced by 9 liters of Liquid B, then the ratio of the two liquids becomes 7 : 9. How much of liquid A was there in the container initially?
A solution contains acid and water in the ratio of 4 : 5. If 20% of the solution is replaced by water, then what will be the ratio of acid and water in the new solution?

A vessel contains some milk. 6 litre of the milk was taken out of the vessel and replaced with 6 litre of water and again 6 litre of mixture was taken out and was replaced with water. Now the ratio of milk to water become 100 : 69. Find the initial quantity of milk in the vessel.

A vessel contained a solution of acid and water, in which water was 64%. Four litres of the solution was taken out of the vessel and the same quantity of water was added. If the resulting solution contains 30% acid, the quantity (in litres) of the water in the solution, at the beginning in the vessel, was:

A vessel contains a solution of two liquids A and B in the ratio 5 : 3. When 10 litres of the solution is taken out and replaced by the same quantity of B, the ratio of A and B in the vessel becomes 10 : 11. The quantity (in litres) of the solution, in the vessel was ____.

A and B are travelling towards each other from the points P and Q respectively. After crossing each other, A and B take p hours and 7 1/5 hours, respectively, to reach their destinations Y and X, respectively. What is the value of p?

A and B are travelling towards each other from the points P and Q, respectively. After crossing each other, A and B took 9 hours and 16 hours to reach their destinations Q and P, respectively. If the speed of A was 56 km/h, then what was the speed (in km/h) of B?

Two trains, A and B started travelling towards each other at the same time, from places P to Q and Q to P, respectively. After crossing each other, A and B took 9 hours and 16 hours to reach Q and P, respectively. If the speed of A was 56 km/h, then what was the speed (in km/h) of B?

A thief is noticed by a policeman from a distance of 200 m. The thief starts running and the policeman chases him. The thief and the policeman run at the rate of 10 km/h and 11 km/h, respectively. What is the distance (in m) between them after 6 min?

A thief is noticed by a policeman from a distance of 80 metres. The thief starts running and the policeman chases him. The thief and policeman run at the rate of 11 km/h and 13 km/h, respectively. After running for how many kilometres will the policeman be able to catch the thief?
An inspector is 288 metres behind a thief. The inspector runs 42 metres and the thief runs 30 metres in a minute. In how much time will the inspector catch the thief?

A policeman is chasing a thief who is 630 metres ahead of him. If they run at a speed of 9.5 km/hr and 6.5 km/hr, respectively, then what distance (in km, rounded off to 1 decimal place) does the thief run before he is nabbed by the policeman?

A thief is noticed by a policeman from a distance of 98 m. The thief starts running and the policeman chases him. The thief and policeman run at the speed of 90 km/h and 118.8 km/h, respectively. What is the time taken to catch the thief by a policeman?

A thief steals an item and escapes, running at 20 km/h. At what distance from the spot of crime would the policeman catch up with the thief, and what is the speed at which the policeman ran?

A thief steals a van at 3:00 a.m. and drives it at a speed of 57 km/h. The theft is discovered at 4:00 a.m. and the owner starts the chase with another van at a speed of 76 km/h. At what time will he catch the thief?

A thief steals an item and escapes, running at a speed of 15 km/h. A policeman arrives at the spot of the crime after 4 minutes and immediately starts chasing the thief. 16 minutes after the policeman started to chase the thief; there is still a gap of 200 m between the two. At what distance from the spot of the crime will the policeman catch up with the thief, and what is the speed (in km/h) of the policeman?

A starts walking at 4 km/hr and after 4 hours, B starts cycling from the same point as that of A, in the same direction at 10 km/hr. After how much distance from the starting point will B catch up with A (Correct to two decimal places)?

The tree is situated at a distance of 7.5 m from Subhas and the building is situated at a distance of 45 m. Moreover, Subhas eyes, the height of the tree and the height of the building are in the same line, and Subhas eyes are 1.8 meters above the floor. Find the height (in m) of the building.

A person was standing on a road near a mall. He was 1425 m away from the mall and able to see the top of the mall from the road in such a way that the top of a tree which is in between him and the mall was exactly in line of sight with the top of the mall. The tree height is 10 m and it is 30 m away from him. How tall (in m) is the mall?

options.B: The angle formed by any side at the incentre is always 90° more than the angle at the opposite vertex.
options.C: Incentre is the point of concurrency of angle bisectors of a triangle.
options.D: Centroid of the triangle divides each median in the ratio of 2:1
